name: Jungfraujoch - Top of Europe
overview: Jungfraujoch is a mountain pass in the Swiss Alps at 3,466 meters, accessible by the Jungfrau Railway. Known as the 'Top of Europe', it offers spectacular alpine views, glaciers, and year-round snow activities.
history: The Jungfrau Railway, completed in 1912, was an engineering marvel carved through the Eiger and Mönch mountains. It remains the highest railway station in Europe and a UNESCO World Heritage site since 2001.
highlights: Sphinx Observatory with panoramic alpine views
Ice Palace with intricate ice sculptures
Aletsch Glacier - longest glacier in the Alps
Alpine Sensation exhibition about railway construction
tips: Dress warmly - temperatures can be -10°C even in summer. Take sunglasses for snow glare. Allow full day for the journey. Book early departure to avoid afternoon crowds and clouds.
